title: Heat pump consumed ~60% more than usual
detail: Sustained elevated heat-pump electricity use over 8 days, beyond what outdoor temperature explains. Possible defrost fault, low refrigerant, or thermostat misconfiguration.
suggested_action: Check heat pump settings / book a service inspection.

title: Cheapest power is around 13:00
detail: Over the last week the lowest average price was at 13:00 (~EUR 0.379/kWh). Shift flexible loads (EV, dishwasher, laundry) into this window.
suggested_action: Schedule EV charging and appliances overnight.

title: Highest bill in January
detail: January was your most expensive month this year, driven by heavy heating demand and minimal solar in winter. Your lowest bill was in June when solar covered most of your needs.
suggested_action: Pre-heat during cheap/sunny hours; review winter heating schedule.

title: Highest bill in January
detail: January was your most expensive month this year due to heavy heating demand and short days reducing solar output. July was your cheapest month thanks to surplus solar.
suggested_action: Pre-heat during cheap/sunny hours; review winter heating schedule.

title: Heat pump consumed ~60% more than usual
detail: Sustained elevated heat-pump electricity use over 8 days, beyond what outdoor temperature explains. Possible defrost fault, low refrigerant, or thermostat misconfiguration.

title: Cheapest power is around 00:00
detail: Over the last week the lowest average price was at 00:00 (~EUR 0.349/kWh). Shift flexible loads (EV, dishwasher, laundry) into this window.

title: Highest bill in January
detail: January was your most expensive month, driven by heating demand and low solar. May was your cheapest month with long sunny days covering most of your consumption.
suggested_action: Pre-heat during cheap/sunny hours; review winter heating schedule.

title: Cheapest power is around 13:00
detail: Over the last week the lowest average price was at 13:00 (~EUR 0.379/kWh). Shift flexible loads (EV, dishwasher, laundry) into this window.
suggested_action: Schedule EV charging and appliances overnight.

title: Highest bill in December
detail: December was your most expensive month — no solar production and high base consumption. July was your cheapest month with surplus solar feeding back to the grid.
suggested_action: Pre-heat during cheap/sunny hours; review winter heating schedule.

title: AC units pushing consumption into the red
detail: Sustained high consumption over the past week — air conditioning is running at full load during afternoon peak hours. Your solar (6.4 kWp) can't cover it, so the gap is coming from the grid at the most expensive time of day.
suggested_action: Set AC to 24°C instead of 20°C, pre-cool the home before 11:00 when solar is peaking, and stagger appliances away from 14:00–19:00.

title: Heat pump consumed ~35% more than usual
detail: Sustained elevated heat-pump electricity use over several days during the cold snap, beyond what the outdoor temperature alone explains. Possible defrost fault, low refrigerant, or a thermostat set too high.
suggested_action: Check heat pump settings / book a service inspection.

title: Heat pump consumed ~75% more than usual
detail: Sustained elevated heat-pump electricity use over several days in February, well beyond what the cold weather explains. Possible defrost fault, low refrigerant, or a thermostat misconfiguration.
suggested_action: Check heat pump settings / book a service inspection.
